introQueue.label: What to push on
introQueue.name: What feels worth pushing on right now?
introQueue.description: Growth-fitted opener. Surfaces what the report is actively choosing to develop, not what's drifting.

introQueue.label: Agenda check
introQueue.name: Before we get into it, anything you want to make sure we cover today?
introQueue.description: Semi-set early question. Gives the report explicit permission to set the agenda before the manager's plan takes over.

introQueue.label: Growth blockers
introQueue.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
introQueue.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

introQueue.label: Direction
introQueue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
introQueue.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

focus_points.label: Next-level stretch in design craft and scope
focus_points.reason: What they want to build next, in concrete design moves, and what would make the next step feel real.

focus_points.label: How critique and feedback are landing
focus_points.reason: Whether feedback they give and receive is improving decisions and quality, or stalling progress — worth clarifying how it’s working in practice.

focus_points.label: Stakeholder influence beyond the design team
focus_points.reason: What they’re doing to shape product decisions through trust and clear tradeoffs, not just deliverables.

focus_points.label: Where their work is moving the business
focus_points.reason: What outcomes they can point to where their design leadership created measurable leverage this cycle.

brief.coreIssue: This growth conversation is likely about what senior-level design growth now means in scope, influence, and visible business impact.
brief.openingQuestion: What kind of design scope would feel like a real next stretch for you this cycle, and where do you want more influence beyond the design team?
listenFor: whether they name a specific project where they want broader ownership or decision authority
whether they volunteer an example of feedback or critique changing a design decision
if they speak concretely about product, engineering, or business stakeholders rather than only design craft
avoid: do not turn the conversation into a vague aspiration chat with no concrete scope or artefact
do not prescribe a growth path before you hear how Sam defines meaningful senior-level stretch
brief.goodOutcome: You and Sam have agreed one senior-designer stretch scope to own end-to-end that requires cross-functional influence, not just stronger craft execution.
brief.suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent project where Sam showed strong craft and one where broader stakeholder influence may have raised the level of impact.

issues: listenFor may lack observable behavioural cue: "whether they volunteer an example of feedback or critique ch…"
listenFor may lack observable behavioural cue: "if they speak concretely about product, engineering, or busi…"

queueRef.label: Early meetings
queueRef.name: Early problem-framing meetings — what would let you earn a seat there?
queueRef.description: Drills into the specific aspiration thread by surfacing what access, trust, or proof Sam thinks is required.

queueRef.label: Current footing
queueRef.name: What's feeling solid in your design work right now, Sam?
queueRef.description: Establishes Sam's present operating base before moving into stretch or change.

queueRef.label: Growth blockers
queueRef.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
queueRef.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

queueRef.label: Tradeoffs ahead
queueRef.name: What would you need to trade off to take on a bigger design scope?
queueRef.description: Forces a real view of time, focus, and competing responsibilities.

queueRef.label: Next move
queueRef.name: What is the first concrete step you want to take before we talk again?
queueRef.description: Closes on one committed action that advances the growth conversation.

I'd be in the early problem-framing meetings, pushing back on scope and tradeoffs, not just executing tickets. I'd own the metric we're movi

question.label: What to push on
question.name: What feels worth pushing on right now?
question.description: Growth-fitted opener. Surfaces what the report is actively choosing to develop, not what's drifting.

transcript.answer: I want to move from just shipping screens to owning a whole product area. I keep getting pulled into pixel polish when I'd rather be shaping the direction with PM and eng earlier.
transcript.note: Sam names a concrete growth direction — owning a whole product area and shaping direction earlier with PM and engineering — showing strong ambition and active investment.

question.label: Own product area
question.name: Owning a whole product area — what would you be doing differently week to week?
question.description: Drills into Sam's stated aspiration so the next-level scope becomes concrete in day-to-day terms.

transcript.answer: I'd be in the early problem-framing meetings, pushing back on scope and tradeoffs, not just executing tickets. I'd own the metric we're moving, not just the Figma file.
transcript.note: Sam described concrete next-level scope—early problem framing, tradeoff influence, and owning metrics—which is a strong positive growth signal.

closer.label: Next move
closer.name: What is the first concrete step you want to take before we talk again?
closer.description: Closes on one committed action that advances the growth conversation.
